items.title: More than shells on the shore: RM7.3mil cocaine found on beach
items.summary: KUANTAN: Police are investigating the discovery of suspected cocaine weighing more than 16kg, with an estimated street value of RM7.31 million, which washed ashore on a beach in Pekan. Pahang police chief Datuk Seri Yahaya Othman said officers were alerted by members of the public to a suspicious bag found at Pantai Tanjung Gosong, Tanjung Selangor, at 2.45pm yesterday. Police said the quantity seized could potentially affect more than 81,000 users.

items.title: HRPB Cardiology team makes history again, wins ACC 2026 world title
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: The Cardiology team from Hospital Raja Permaisuri Bainun (HRPB), Ipoh has once again brought pride to Malaysia by emerging as world champions in the Fellows-in-Training (FITs) Jeopardy competition at the American College of Cardiology (ACC) Conference 2026 in New Orleans. The achievement was secured through the combined efforts of three young Malaysian specialists, Dr Chen Tai Meng, Dr Ang Jian-Gang and Dr Yee Shen Yew, who outperformed participants from various countries. "Congratulations also to Dr Nor Hanim Mohd Amin (Head of HRPB'S Cardiology Department) and the entire HRPB Cardiology team for their strong leadership and support.

items.title: Apex court dismisses NGO's appeal over Bukit Cherakah degazettement
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA: The Federal Court, in a 2-1 majority decision, dismissed an appeal by two non-governmental organisations (NGOs) challenging the degazettement of Bukit Cherakah as a forest reserve. Court of Appeal President Datuk Seri Abu Bakar Jais and Federal Court Judge Datuk Rhodzariah Bujang formed the majority in dismissing the appeal, while Federal Court Judge Datuk Lee Swee Seng delivered a dissenting judgment. In 2022, the Shah Alam High Court denied the NGOs leave to commence a judicial review of the Selangor government's decision to degazette 406.22ha of Bukit Cherakah.

items.title: Court defers proceedings in murder case against Nepali for interpreter
items.summary: BUKIT MERTAJAM: The magistrate's court here today deferred proceedings against a Nepali man charged with murdering his co-worker to allow for the appointment of an interpreter. In the 1.10am incident, the victim sustained fatal stab wounds and was later pronounced dead while receiving treatment at Bukit Mertajam Hospital. Preliminary findings revealed stab wounds to the victim's ribs.

items.title: Index crime drops during Hari Raya, say police
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Index crime declined during the Hari Raya Aidilfitri period, says Federal police Crime Prevention and Community Safety Department director Datuk Seri Mohd Yusri Hassan Basri. "Comparisons between Op Selamat 24, held from March 29 to April 3, 2025, and Op Selamat 26, from March 19 to 24 this year, showed a rise in the use of the VSP complaint module from 80 cases to 150. Yusri added that Op Selamat 26 recorded 61,122 visits to unattended homes and 31,636 mobile patrol vehicle operations, compared with Op Selamat 24, which saw 76,274 visits and 37,429 patrols carried out.

items.title: Police appeal for leads on two individuals missing since March 25 and 26 in Nibong Tebal
items.summary: NIBONG TEBAL, April 1 — Police are actively trying to locate two individuals who have been reported missing after leaving their respective homes on March 25 and 26, and have not returned since. Seberang Perai Selatan police chief Supt Jay January Siowou said that the first individual is a 44-year-old man, Lim Hock Seng, who has been missing since March 25. “The other is a woman, Nur Ain Ariffen, 39, who has been reported missing since last week.

items.title: WHO chief praises Malaysia's role in strengthening global health cooperation
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA: The Director-General of the World Health Organisation, Tedros Adhanom Ghebreyesus, has praised Malaysia for its commitment and active engagement in international health issues, as well as its role in strengthening global cooperation. Malaysia's Health Ministry said the acknowledgement came during Dr Tedros' official visit, reflecting what it described as a close and sustained partnership between Malaysia and the WHO in enhancing health system preparedness and resilience. Meanwhile, Health Minister Datuk Seri Dr Dzulkefly Ahmad said Malaysia's active participation in the WHO ensures the country's voice remains represented in the global health landscape.

items.title: Widow seeks 'justice' as husband's alleged drunk driver charged with murder
items.summary: KLANG: Although the court proceedings lasted just over five minutes, seeing the man accused of killing her husband formally charged with murder gave Nor Nadia Abdul Majid a sense of justice. After the proceedings, Nadia became tearful as officers from the Klang Selatan Traffic Enforcement and Investigation Department spoke with her. Nadia, whose husband Amirul Hafiz Omar, 32, was buried just two days ago, said she is preparing to return to her hometown in Taiping, Perak.

items.title: No notice filed for Azam Baki rally, say police
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Police have yet to receive any notification from organisers of the "Tangkap Azam Baki" rally, scheduled to be held at Dataran Merdeka on April 25. Dang Wangi police chief Assistant Commissioner Sazalee Adam said that, as of today, no application or notification had been submitted to the authorities. On Monday, the Tangkap Azam Baki Secretariat announced plans to hold the rally to demand a transparent investigation into the share ownership issue involving Malaysian Anti-Corruption Commission (MACC) chief commissioner Tan Sri Azam Baki, as well as allegations of corporate mafia influence within enforcement agencies.

items.title: Higher Education Ministry urges industry to tap RM5m Public Private Research Network fund to boost productivity
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 1 — The Ministry of Higher Education Malaysia (MOHE) has called on industry players to take advantage of the RM5 million fund provided under the Public Private Research Network (PPRN) initiative to enhance company productivity. The ministry’s Deputy Secretary-General (Policy) Datuk Dr Megat Sany Megat Ahmad Supian said the initiative, which began in 2015, has recorded the implementation of more than 700 innovation projects involving collaborations between public and private universities and industry players. He said the initiative aims to cultivate knowledge transfer and expertise from researchers in higher education institutions (HEIs) through research collaborations with local companies that may lack expertise and capital.

items.title: Four-year-old boy undergoes 50 rounds of chemotherapy in leukaemia battle
items.summary: Iffat Qaleef Mohamad Iqbal Akhim's condition first became apparent when he suffered frequent fevers before losing the ability to walk due to severe leg pain. His parents, Nur Qamarina Noor Azmi, 26, and Mohamad Iqbal Akhim Mahat, 30, initially sought traditional treatment, but the relief was short-lived. Nur Qamarina said the financial strain had been overwhelming.

items.title: 'My child blames herself': Court hears emotional account in Zara inquest
items.summary: KOTA KINABALU: A mother told the Coroner's Court that her child became emotional, distressed and was often sobbing when recounting an incident involving the late Zara Qairina Mahathir. Her child said Zara had jumped from the hostel building and asked her to pray for her. However, she said her child did not provide further details at the time.

items.title: Woman killed after car skids off road and hits palm tree
items.summary: KOTA TINGGI: A 36-year-old woman was killed after her car lost control and veered off Km2 of Jalan Lok Heng-Mawai, crashing into a palm tree earlier today. District police chief Superintendent Yusof Othman said the driver sustained severe internal injuries and was pronounced dead at the scene. Yusof urged all motorists to remain vigilant and adhere to traffic regulations to ensure road safety for everyone.

items.title: Police seek public help to find missing 15‑year‑old girl in Mukah
items.summary: =MUKAH, April 1 — Police are appealing to the public to help locate a 15-year-old girl who went missing yesterday. Mukah police chief DSP Muhamad Rizal Alias said Angel Daina from Jalan Tiong Hua here was last seen wearing a pink short-sleeved T-shirt and long black pants. “Anyone who knows her whereabouts is advised to contact the nearest police station or the Mukah police station on 084-871222,” he added.

items.title: Convenience store worker pleads guilty to RM32,000 theft to settle Ah Long debts
items.summary: BATU PAHAT: A convenience store worker was charged at the Sessions Court here today with seven counts of criminal breach of trust (CBT), involving more than RM32,000 allegedly used to settle debts with illegal moneylenders. The 23-year-old Nurfatin Najwa Ahmad Bzli pleaded guilty to all charges before Judge Osman Affendi Mohd Shalleh. She is accused of misappropriating RM32,101.70 from daily sales proceeds belonging to the store.

items.title: Malaysian vehicles among foreign cars required to have three‑quarters full tank to enter Brunei from April 1
items.summary: MIRI, April 1 — Malaysian vehicle owners are advised to fill before entering Brunei, as its government will require all foreign vehicles to have at least three-quarters of a tank of fuel starting April 1. “All foreign-registered vehicles entering Brunei Darussalam will be required to maintain a minimum fuel level of three-quarter tank upon entry. “Foreign vehicles that do not comply with this requirement will not be allowed to enter Brunei Darussalam,” the statement said.

items.title: Cloud seeding to boost dam levels as heatwave grips parts of Malaysia
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA: The National Disaster Management Agency (Nadma) will carry out four rounds of cloud seeding operations from April 6 to 9 in Perak, Kedah, Perlis and Johor to boost water levels in dams affected by prolonged hot weather. "These conditions have not significantly increased dam water levels, affecting water resources, the agricultural sector and daily life, particularly for vulnerable communities in rural and interior areas," said the chairman of the Central Disaster Management Committee in a statement. "This measure is a proactive effort to restore water levels affected by prolonged hot weather," Zahid said.

items.title: PM Anwar's phone-call diplomacy wins safe Hormuz access
items.summary: Few outcomes are as strategically significant as Iran allowing Malaysian vessels to pass through the Straits of Hormuz. According to news reports, about 50 per cent of Malaysia's total oil passes through the narrow strip. Malaysia has always strongly defended its neutrality.

items.title: Government withdraws appeal on Pastor Koh report; court awards RM15,000 costs to family
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA, April 1 — The government today withdrew its appeal against a High Court ruling granting Pastor Raymond Koh’s family access to a classified report. As reported by Free Malaysia Today (FMT), senior federal counsel Nurul Farhana Khalid informed the Court of Appeal that a notice of discontinuation had been filed, leading the panel to strike out the appeal. “However, lawyer Michelle Wong objected, arguing that substantial work had gone into responding to the appeal.” “She sought RM15,000 in costs, pointing out that an application to introduce fresh evidence was filed last month.” The appellate court, chaired by Justice Supang Lian alongside Justices Ismail Brahim and K Muniandy, awarded RM15,000 in costs to Koh’s family despite the government’s request to waive it.

items.title: Two teens drown during morning swim at Papar riverside campsite
items.summary: PAPAR, April 1 — A recreational swim at a riverside campsite here ended in tragedy when two teenagers drowned at Pokdi Campsite in Mandalipau here yesterday morning. Papar Fire and Rescue (Bomba) Station chief Asst Fire Supt Rozlan Osman said firefighters were dispatched to the location after receiving a distress call at 10.10am. He said members of the public managed to pull both victims from the river before firefighters arrived at the scene.

items.title: Customs crackdown: RM2.4mil in smuggled rice, meat, and e-bikes seized
items.summary: IPOH: The Perak Customs Department has cracked down on a string of smuggling attempts involving rice, frozen meat, tiles, and electric bicycles, with total duties and taxes estimated at RM2.4 million. In one operation, he said officers from the Teluk Intan branch inspected a container at Port Klang on Jan 14 and uncovered 801 cartons falsely declared as tiles. He said another operation on Jan 27 saw 765 sacks of rice, weighing 22.9 tonnes, seized at Port Klang after being falsely declared as fried gram.

items.title: Johor KPDN ramps up petrol station checks as RON95 ban begins
items.summary: JOHOR BARU: The Johor Domestic Trade and Cost of Living Ministry (KPDN) has stepped up monitoring at all 639 petrol stations in the state following the enforcement of the RON95 petrol sales ban on foreign-registered vehicles, effective today. State director Lilis Saslinda Pornomo said heightened surveillance would focus on 31 petrol stations near the Singapore border to prevent fuel leakages. Monitoring is being conducted at all petrol stations, particularly in border areas and other high-risk locations, with over 180 enforcement officers stationed across Johor," she said when contacted today.

items.title: Police shoot dead armed man who allegedly killed his younger brother in Tenom
items.summary: TENOM, April 1 — A man was shot dead by police after he allegedly shot and killed his younger brother with a bakakuk (homemade shotgun) in an incident at Kampung Kangkawang at Jalan Kemabong here on Sunday. Tenom District Police Chief Supt Azmir Abd Razak said the 57-year-old suspect died at the scene after police opened fire, striking him in the right arm, left chest and left rib. “In self-defence, police fired three shots that hit the suspect in the right arm, left chest and left rib.

items.title: Flight costs covered: MACC tells James Chai to return in RM1.1b ARM probe
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA, April 1 — The Malaysian Anti‑Corruption Commission (MACC) has offered to pay for political analyst James Chai’s flight back to Malaysia as part of its investigation into alleged misconduct tied to a government‑backed semiconductor project worth RM1.11 billion with UK‑based ARM Holdings. MACC said the probe is “actively ongoing”, with statements already taken from 17 witnesses including Economy Ministry officers and Malaysian Investment Development Authority (Mida) officials. Chai Jin Shern, better known as James Chai, has been identified as a required witness.

items.title: Dispel myth of 'healthy' alcohol consumption
items.summary: LETTERS: We often reassure ourselves that alcohol is only a marginal issue in Malaysia. After all, just 10.4 per cent of Malaysians report imbibing alcohol, according to the National Health and Morbidity Survey (NHMS) 2023. In other words, Malaysia has a dangerous alcohol consumption problem.

items.title: Where is Pamela Ling? Family seeks answers nearly a year after her disappearance
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: The family of Pamela Ling, who disappeared nearly a year ago, has called for urgent action and accountability from authorities, as investigations into her disappearance remain unresolved. The family, through their lawyer, Sangeet Kaur Deo, said the investigation seemed to lack direction and seriousness from the outset, leaving her family without answers. She added that repeated interrogations, prolonged travel restrictions, and alleged heavy-handed conduct by the Malaysian Anti-Corruption Commission (MACC) preceded Ling's disappearance.

items.title: [UPDATED] MACC offers to fly in James Chai as RM1.1bil Arm probe intensifies
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA: The Malaysian Anti-Corruption Commission (MACC) has offered to cover travel costs for James Chai to return to Malaysia to assist in investigations into alleged misconduct linked to a RM1.1 billion government deal with Arm Holdings signed in March last year. In a statement today, MACC said investigators have also obtained key documents, including agreements and supporting materials, and are expected to record statements from a further eight individuals, including those currently overseas. The MACC said Chai, also known as Chai Jin Shern, is required to assist in the investigation.

items.title: Johor MB courts Indian professionals as state sharpens economic push
items.summary: JOHOR BARU: Johor Menteri Besar Datuk Onn Hafiz Ghazi has stepped up engagement with the Indian professional community, positioning them as a strategic partner in the state's economic ambitions. Onn Hafiz said the state government was acting in line with the call of Tunku Mahkota Ismail, Regent of Johor, who has consistently stressed the importance of safeguarding the people's welfare. On Sunday (March 29), Onn Hafiz held an engagement session with Indian professionals in Johor, spanning sectors such as business, education, engineering, law and industry.

items.title: #NSTviral: Tears of pride: Fried banana seller weeps as son scores 11A+ in SPM [WATCH]
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: A father was overcome with emotion as his son received his SPM results after scoring 11A+. The clip shows his father, Zainudin Abdul Latif, holding his son's face and crying tears of joy. @tvpss.smatkem Tahniah Fakhrul Iman atas kecemerlangan #spm2025 #batch08 #smatkem #straightaplus original sound - TV PSS SMA TELOK KEMANG Zainudin, a fried banana seller in Telok Kemang, appeared visibly moved by his son's success.

items.title: MyNews poised for earnings rebound on aggressive expansion, product push: CIMB Securities
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: MyNews Holdings Bhd is expected to see a rebound in earnings in the second half of financial year 2026 (FY26), driven by an ambitious store rollout and enhanced product offerings, according to CIMB Securities Sdn Bhd. It said that MyNews's first-quarter core net profit (CNP) came in at RM4.3 million, missing expectations as ongoing store expansion plans led to higher-than-expected operating expenses. However, MyNews's CNP is projected to grow 20.9 per cent year-on-year in FY26, supported by new store openings, improved operating leverage and a more profitable sales mix.

items.title: Ramanan: Two seasoned professionals join Skills Development Fund Corp board to drive skills‑training governance
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 1 — Human Resources Minister Datuk Seri R. Ramanan announced that the appointment of two experienced individuals to the Board of Directors (BOD) of the Skills Development Fund Corporation (PTPK) is part of an effort to strengthen the nation’s human capital development through more strategic and high-impact skills training. Ramanan said that the appointments are in line with the government’s commitment to ensure that PTPK continues to play a central role as a key institution in supporting the national skills training ecosystem, including expanding access to training financing and enhancing the employability of graduates from Technical and Vocational Education and Training (TVET). Ramelle Ashram has extensive experience in the corporate sector, particularly in the fields of skills training and talent development.

items.title: Police seek help to locate woman missing in Penang
items.summary: GEORGE TOWN: Police are seeking public assistance to locate a 39-year-old woman who went missing after leaving her home last week. Seberang Prai Selatan police chief Superintendent Jay January Siowou identified the woman as Nur Ain Ariffen, who was reported missing on March 26. Jay said information can be conveyed to investigating officer Sergeant Major T. Kangeswari at 010-375 0353 or at any police station.

items.title: Malaysia's financial system resilient despite external headwinds: RHB Research
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Malaysia's financial system remains resilient despite rising external risks, with businesses and households entering a potentially more challenging environment from a position of strength, according to RHB Research. This follows the briefing by Bank Negara Malaysia in conjunction with the release of its Financial Stability Review for the second half of 2025 (2H25) on Tuesday. Meanwhile, the central bank's assessment showed that business activity in the second half of 2025 remained supported by resilient domestic demand.

items.title: MCMC probes three for circulating fake electricity‑tariff claims with edited news graphics on social media
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA, April 1 — The Malaysian Communications and Multimedia Commission (MCMC) has launched an investigation into three individuals suspected of spreading false information about a new electricity tariff increase on digital platforms. “Based on complaints received, the content disseminated by the individuals regarding the electricity tariff increase is false and capable of creating negative perceptions, leading to anxiety and panic among the public. MCMC stressed that it takes seriously any misuse of digital platforms aimed at misleading the public through the spread of false information.

items.title: Dang Wangi police on alert ahead of Azam Baki rally
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Dang Wangi police are closely monitoring plans for a rally by the group "Sekretariat Tangkap Azam Baki" at Dataran Merdeka on April 25, even as no organisers have formally notified the authorities of the proposed gathering. Dang Wangi police chief Assistant Commissioner Sazalee Adam said checks so far showed that no organisers had formally notified the authorities of the gathering. Sekretariat Tangkap Azam Baki said both issues remained unresolved, raising concerns about the integrity of the MACC and national enforcement institutions.

items.title: New max price guide: If you rent a tour bus, tour van now in Malaysia, you might pay…
items.summary: Here’s the new maximum rental price for tour buses The Malaysian Inbound Tourism Association (Mita) on Monday announced its new guide on the maximum that can be charged to rent a 44-seat tour bus, which will change depending on the diesel prices in Malaysia. There are two categories: Bus (Domestic Day Tour): You are renting the entire tour bus for only a day trip within Malaysia. Bus (Series tour): You are renting the entire tour bus for multiple days, usually travelling across different states in Malaysia.

items.title: Six-vehicle pile-up in Kampar not caused by drink-driver
items.summary: The 6.42pm crash left five people injured, including two siblings aged five and seven who sustained minor head injuries. District police chief Superintendent Mohamad Nazri Daud said initial investigations found no signs of intoxication in the driver, believed to have triggered the collision. Nazri said the accident involved five cars and a motorcycle.

items.title: Murder trial of Myanmar nationals postponed over language barrier
items.summary: GEORGE TOWN: Proceedings against two Myanmar nationals charged with murdering a fellow countryman last month were postponed at the magistrate's court here today after it emerged that both men do not understand Malay. Deputy public prosecutor Siti Marissa Safina Saiful Adli requested the court to set a date for the appointment of a Myanmar-language interpreter to ensure the charge could be properly communicated. Magistrate Tunku Intan Nadiah Tunku Mohammed Nazarin fixed April 23 for the interpreter to be appointed.

items.title: JB High Court dismisses foreign shipping firm’s suit over crew detention and cargo seizure
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA, April 1 — The Johor Bahru High Court has dismissed a lawsuit filed by a foreign shipping company against the government and police over the alleged wrongful detention of its crew and seizure of cargo five years ago. In his grounds of judgment dated March 26, Judge Datuk Seri Shamsulbahri Ibrahim ruled that Blue Ocean Shipping Limited had no locus standi to bring a claim for wrongful detention on behalf of the crew of MV Chita 2. He said any claim for wrongful detention should have been filed by the crew members themselves.

items.title: Sabah sets up tourism crisis unit amid global uncertainty
items.summary: KOTA KINABALU: The Sabah Tourism Board (STB) will establish a dedicated crisis response unit to safeguard the state's tourism industry amid growing global uncertainties. The decision was made during an STB board meeting chaired by Assistant Minister to the Chief Minister and STB chairman Datuk Joniston Bangkuai yesterday, following concerns over external factors that could impact travel demand, airline operations and market stability. Joniston said that while Sabah's tourism industry is on a recovery path, the global operating environment is becoming increasingly volatile.

items.title: Family calls off wedding, suffers RM150,000 loss to keep daughter safe
items.summary: KOTA BARU: The wedding of cosmetic entrepreneur Fara Sharina Zamari's daughter, Putri Fasha Felisa Mohd Izhan, 19, was called off after the teenager was assaulted by her then-fiancé. Fara Sharina, 37, said the ceremony, originally scheduled for yesterday, was cancelled upon learning of the incident, which left her daughter injured. "All the wedding preparations were almost ready, including my daughter's choice of an English-style theme at Padang Polo, Kemumin.

items.title: [UPDATED] Annas Sulaiman appointed as new Wangsa Maju police chief
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Crime buster Assistant Commissioner Annas Sulaiman has been appointed the new Wangsa Maju police chief. The handing-over-of-duties ceremony was witnessed by Kuala Lumpur police chief Datuk Fadil Marsus this morning at the Kuala Lumpur police headquarters. Annas' previous roles include chief of the Ampang Jaya Criminal Investigation Division, deputy police chief of Cheras, and Director of Integrated Operations at the National Financial Crime Prevention Centre (NFCC).

items.title: Malaysia, Saudi Arabia to deepen cooperation on Haj and umrah management, says minister
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 1 — Malaysia and Saudi Arabia will continue to strengthen strategic cooperation in the management of Haj, umrah and related affairs to ensure high-quality services for pilgrims, says Minister in the Prime Minister’s Department (Religious Affairs) Dr Zulkifli Hasan. He said the matter was discussed during his courtesy call on Saudi Arabia’s Minister of Haj and Umrah, Dr Tawfiq Fawzan Al-Rabiah. The meeting was held at the King Salman International Convention Centre in Madinah Al-Munawwarah in conjunction with the Umrah and Ziyarah Forum 2026.

items.title: Driver at centre of viral Klang crash faces murder charge, pleads guilty to drug offence
items.summary: KLANG, April 1 — The driver at the centre of a viral crash that hurled a motorcyclist onto another car was brought before the Magistrates’ Court here today to face a murder charge and a separate drug offence. According to Sinar Harian (SH), R. Saktygaanapathy, 28, nodded to indicate he understood when the murder charge was read before Magistrate A. Karthiyayini. Separately, Saktygaanapathy pleaded guilty to a charge of self‑administering drugs believed to be benzodiazepines and tetrahydrocannabinol (THC), SH reported.

items.title: Two more medical experts to testify in Zara Qairina inquest
items.summary: KOTA KINABALU, April 1 — The inquest proceedings into the death of Zara Qairina Mahathir will continue with testimony from two more expert witnesses, comprising medical specialists, in sessions scheduled to take place in April. Lawyer Shahlan Jufri, representing Zara Qairina’s mother, said the next witness to be called was Dr Chua Sze Huang, a psychiatrist at Hospital Mesra, Bukit Padang here, who is set to testify this Thursday. He said Dr Chua was expected to present findings from an 84-page psychiatric report on Zara Qairina’s condition prior to the tragic incident on July 16 last year.

items.title: New child witness provides new chronology in inquest into Zara Qairina’s death
items.summary: Shahlan Jufri, lawyer representing Zara Qairina’s mother, said the 65th child witness, a senior student at Sekolah Menengah Kebangsaan Agama Tun Datu Mustapha, Papar, presented a new account and chronology of the incident involving the teenager on July 16 last year before Coroner Amir Shah Amir Hassan. This year, a child witness came forward with a new story, a totally different chronology of events. Joan said the mother of the child witness would also give her testimony tomorrow.

items.title: KLCC gets tighter security as DBKL, police deploy smart cameras and panic buttons
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 1 — Kuala Lumpur City Hall (DBKL) has stepped up public safety measures around KLCC following recent incidents and unlicensed activities in the area. Working with the police, DBKL has installed smart closed circuit television (CCTV) cameras to strengthen monitoring and provide a safer environment for visitors. DBKL said the measures reflect its commitment to ensuring Kuala Lumpur remains a safe and comfortable city for residents and tourists.

items.title: TNB announces April automatic fuel adjustment rate with slightly lower electricity rebate
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 1 — Tenaga Nasional Bhd has announced the Automatic Fuel Adjustment (AFA) rate for April, with consumers set to receive a slightly reduced rebate of 0.47 sen per kilowatt hour (kWh). In a post on X, TNB said the AFA does not apply to domestic customers with electricity usage of 600 kWh and below, which corresponds to a bill of approximately RM215.98. The utility firm also revealed the three-month AFA rate forecast for May (39 sen kWh), June (91 sen kWh) and July (8.0 sen kWh).

items.title: Widow demands retribution as suspect in fatal Klang drink-driving crash brought to court
items.summary: KLANG, April 1 — A man in his 20s was brought to court today over a fatal drink‑driving crash on Jalan Raya Barat. According to the New Straits Times (NST), the suspect was escorted under tight security from the South Klang police station to the court complex, where he is expected to appear before magistrate A. Karthiyayini. He was brought to court a day before his four day remand period was due to expire, NST reported.

items.title: Anwar’s phone‑call diplomacy wins safe transit for Malaysian vessels through Strait of Hormuz (VIDEO)
items.summary: Few outcomes are as strategically significant as Iran allowing Malaysian vessels to pass through the Straits of Hormuz. According to news reports, about 50 per cent of Malaysia’s total oil passes through the narrow strip. Malaysia has always strongly defended its neutrality.

items.title: Three detained in Johor over TikTok content alleged to insult Islam and Johor royal institution
items.summary: JOHOR BAHRU, April 1 — Police have detained three individuals, including a 17-year-old teenager, to assist in an investigation into the dissemination of content on the TikTok application containing elements of incitement, defamation, and insults against Islam, the Johor royal institution, and the Johor police chief. Johor police chief Datuk Ab Rahaman Arsad said that the two women and one man, aged between 17 and 49, were arrested in Taman Saujana here early this morning. The content in the post included claims that the Sultan of Johor and the Tengku Mahkota Johor, as well as the Prime Minister, sought advice from the account owner in preparing speech texts.

items.title: Police seek motorcyclist who assaulted driver and smashed car door in Jasin
items.summary: Jasin district police chief Supt Lee Robert said the victim, a man, 35, lodged a report about the incident at 3.34 pm on March 26, stating that he had seen a man riding a blue Yamaha Legend behind his car who looked like he was going to fall over. “When the suspect overtook the victim’s car on the right, the victim lowered his window and confronted the suspect, leading the man to turn back towards the car in an angered state,” he said in a statement here yesterday, adding that the suspect then kicked the car’s driver’s side door and used his white helmet to hit the door and window three or four times till the door was dented. The victim then came down from the car and the suspect hit him, causing his right wrist to sustain minor injuries.

items.title: Malaysia, WHO ramp up cooperation to improve early detection of infectious diseases, says health minister
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 1 — Cooperation between Malaysia and the World Health Organisation (WHO) will strengthen the country’s preparedness and early warning systems against infectious disease threats. Health Minister Datuk Seri Dr Dzulkefly Ahmad said this is being advanced through key global negotiations, including the Pathogen Access and Benefit Sharing (PABS) mechanism under the Intergovernmental Working Group (IGWG), amendments to the International Health Regulations (IHR) and the Pandemic Agreement. He said the meeting reflected the close global health diplomacy between Malaysia and WHO, with Dr Tedros expressing appreciation for Malaysia’s active role in addressing international health issues.

items.title: MCMC calls for submissions under Jendela 2 as Malaysia moves to expand nationwide connectivity infrastructure
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 1 — The Malaysian Communications and Multimedia Commission (MCMC) has issued its first invitation for the submission of draft plans under the National Digital Network Phase 2 (JENDELA 2), reflecting the government’s continued commitment to strengthening digital infrastructure and enhancing connectivity nationwide. “Group 1 covers 1,000 sites nationwide, with 31.1 per cent in Peninsular Malaysia, 33.7 per cent in Sarawak and 35.2 per cent in Sabah. Since the implementation of JENDELA Phase 1, MCMC said the government had made progress in expanding coverage and improving service quality, while JENDELA 2 continued these efforts.

items.title: Why your morning commute was hit: Overgrown tree disrupts Kelana Jaya LRT line
items.summary: Trains from Putra Heights terminated at Kelana Jaya, while trains from Gombak ended their journey at Taman Jaya. Gangguan Laluan Kelana Jaya berikutan halangan dahan pokok di landasan berhampiran Taman Paramount. pic.twitter.com/bNAWFTw0EI — Ask Rapid KL (@askrapidkl) April 1, 2026 A shuttle train was deployed to ferry passengers between the Kelana Jaya and Taman Jaya stations.

items.title: Malaysia couple accused in Melbourne of enslaving Indonesian woman, abusing her and treating her like property
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 1 — An Indonesian woman was allegedly beaten, starved and forced to sleep on stairs or in a garage while working as a “slave” for a Malaysian couple, an Australian court heard. The Victorian County Court was told the ordeal lasted months after the woman agreed to stay with Malaysian nationals Chee Kit Chong and Angie Yeh Liaw at their Point Cook home in early 2022, as reported by the ABC Australia. “Mr Chong threatened and coerced [the alleged victim] to provide domestic services,” prosecutor Shaun Ginsbourg S.C. reportedly told the court.

items.title: Police seek four men over machete attack that left man seriously hurt in Bera
items.summary: KUANTAN, April 1 — Police are tracking down four men to assist in investigations into a machete attack that left a man seriously injured at a shop in Jalan Besar Mengkuang, Triang, Bera. Bera police chief Supt Zulkiflee Nazir said police received information on the incident involving the 35-year-old victim at 12.46 am yesterday. “The incident, believed to have occurred at about 9 pm last Sunday, left the victim seriously injured and he was taken to Temerloh Hospital for further treatment,” he said in a statement tonight.

items.title: Amir Hamzah urges Malaysians to adopt prudent, moderate lifestyle amid global uncertainties
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 1 — The government has urged the public to adopt a more prudent and moderate lifestyle in facing global economic uncertainties, said Finance Minister II Datuk Seri Amir Hamzah Azizan. He said this after chairing the Third Meeting of the Special Committee on Energy Security and the Impact of the West Asia Conflict yesterday, which discussed the findings of the National Economic Action Council (MTEN) meeting. Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim also said today that the Madani government will continue to act proactively based on data and current realities, including obtaining views from industry players and small and medium enterprises (SMEs), in addressing the global energy crisis.

items.title: Malaysia posts 5.2pc growth in 2025, with lowest inflation in five years, showing govt policies are working, says Anwar
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 1 — Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim said Malaysia’s positive economic performance reflects that the government’s policies are on the right track. Anwar, who is also the Finance Minister, said this can be seen in the Bank Negara Malaysia Annual Report 2025 released yesterday, which highlighted the country’s strong and resilient economic fundamentals as well as good governance. “The national economy recorded growth of 5.2 per cent in 2025, supported by resilient domestic demand.

items.title: Anwar says bold reforms needed to rebuild Malaysia’s economic foundations
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 1 — Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim says Malaysia must continue undertaking reforms to rebuild its economic foundations and remain competitive globally. “We need to focus on what makes a real difference, but do not expect all changes to happen at the same time,” he said when moderating Forum Ilmuwan Malaysia Madani (FIM) Series 9 held at Sasana Kijang, Bank Negara Malaysia (BNM) here, last night. The forum featured a panel comprising Finance Minister II Datuk Seri Amir Hamzah Azizan, BNM Governor Datuk Seri Abdul Rasheed Ghaffour, and World Bank Lead Economist for Malaysia, Dr Apurva Sanghi.
